What would theOlympicsbe without some good old meme-esque content? EnterArno Kammingaof the Netherlands, who competed in the Men’s 100m Breaststroke during Day 1 ofswimmingand his “thirst trap” swimshorts. Finishing second place didn’t matter, as he won social media’s devoted attention.



Competing at theOlympic Games Paris 2024at Paris La Defense Arena on Saturday (July 27) in Nanterre, France, the 28-year-old athlete rocked a tightly fitted, glamorous pair of trunks.

In a decision that was clearly made by a public relations team, the Netherlands has opted for the most flesh-colored orange on the thighs of their trunks,News.co.aureported on Monday (July 29).

As a result, theswimwearcreated an optical illusion that made Arno appear almost naked, leaving people stunned on social media, as a person wrote on X (formerly known as Twitter): “Definitely watching for the sport.”

Someone commented: “Name???”

“IN PUBLIC? IS THIS LEGAL?” a separate individual chimed in.

Dutch swimmer at the 2024 Olympics wearing revealing trunks, smiling by the pool.



Image credits:arnokamminga





A post shared by Arno Kamminga (@arnokamminga)

Up until the 1996 Atlanta Games, swimmers typically wore suits that covered as little of their skin as necessary,NBC Olympicsexplained in February 2024.

Shaving one’s entire body was the primary way to enhance speed through the water. Since then, advancements by manufacturers have created a wholesale change in the looks ofOlympic swimmers.

Atlanta marked the beginning of the “bodysuit revolution,” the outlet stated. At those Games, neck-to-knee swimsuits made their Olympic debut, with Speedo’s model, the Aquablade, worn by several medal winners.

Dutch swimmer with a flag at Eiffel Tower during 2024 Olympics.



Image credits:arnokamminga

Prior to the 2000 Sydney Games, when swimmers began wearing suits that covered all parts of their body except their feet, hands, and head, some observers felt the suits were performance-enhancing and, therefore, against the rules.





A post shared by Arno Kamminga (@arnokamminga)

As perNBC, the World Aquatics, swimming’s governing body, declined to ban the suits and, as a result, most competitors inSydneywore some variation of the bodysuit. Most gold medalists at the 2004 Games wore Speedo’s Fastskin suit.

For the Beijing Games, Speedo introduced the LZR Racer suit, which was touted as the world’s fastest swimsuit, the American broadcaster noted.

Made from a compressive, water-repellent, and chlorine-resistant fabric, it was said to reduce drag and provide the most streamlined shape.

Dutch swimmer at 2024 Olympics wearing striking trunks, surrounded by spectators and preparing to compete.



Image credits:Andre Weening/BSR Agency/Getty Images

The Dutch swimmer finished in second place in his Men’s 100m Breaststroke event, recording a time of 59.12,The Spunreported on Sunday (July 28).

All jokes aside, Arno expressed his immense disappointment in his result, writing alongside a photograph of himself shared onInstagramduring the competition: “Not what I came for,” with a broken heart emoji.

Ahead of the competition, the Katwijk native described a case that saw 23 Chinese swimmers test positive for a banned drug as a “lose-lose situation” while also questioning how the investigation was handled by the authorities ahead of the Olympic Games in Paris.





A post shared by Arno Kamminga (@arnokamminga)



The World Anti-Doping Agency (WADA) confirmed reports in April that 23Chineseswimmers tested positive for trimetazidine, a medication that increases blood flow to the heart, before the Tokyo Olympics in 2020,Reutersreported on July 18.

However, WADA reportedly accepted the findings of a Chinese investigation that this was due to substance contamination.

Arno, who won silver medals at the Tokyo Olympics in the 100m and 200m Breaststroke, said that even if due process was followed, the fact that “they didn’t tell anyone” about the positive tests until April was a bone of contention.

Dutch swimmer at the 2024 Olympics in revealing trunks on poolside, sparking viral attention.



Image credits:itsonlyzach

He toldReuters: “Everyone is questioning it, so it’s a lose-lose situation for everyone – for swimming lovers but also for the Chinese swimmers themselves.

“I think it’s hard to judge or say anything without knowing everything.”

“The problem is the way in which it was handled.

Dutch swimmer in revealing trunks at the 2024 Olympics poolside, wearing an orange jacket and swimming cap.



Image credits: Rene Nijhuis/BSR Agency/Getty Images

“For them to just cover it up, not say anything instead of being upfront and transparent with us, I think that’s where the problem lies for most people.

“Whether they’re positive or not, I don’t know.

“I can’t say anything about it. I hope I can trust WADA that the conclusion was right… I’m just disappointed in the way it was handled.”

Dutch swimmer in revealing trunks at the 2024 Olympics poolside, attracting viral attention.



Image credits:Ian_MJ1

While Arno’s trunks ignited amusement, other Olympic sports gear drew criticism, as exemplified by Team USA’s controversial track and field uniforms.

The risqué apparel caused quite a stir back in Aprilafter being unveiled by Nike, with many slamming the decision to adopt the uniforms of sexism and concern over practicality.

At the time, Lauren Fleshman, a retired US world champion runner, took to herInstagram pageto share her concerns, writing: “I’m sorry, but show me one WNBA or NWSL team who would enthusiastically support this kit.

Dutch swimmer in revealing trunks during freestyle race at the 2024 Olympics.



Image credits:Adam Pretty/Getty Images

“This is for Olympic Track and Field. Professional athletes should be able to compete without dedicating brain space to constant pube vigilance or the mental gymnastics of having every vulnerable piece of your body on display.

“Women’s kits should be in service to performance, mentally and physically. If this outfit was truly beneficial to physical performance, men would wear it.

“This is not an elite athletic kit for track and field. This is a costume born of patriarchal forces that are no longer welcome or needed to get eyes on women’s sports.





A post shared by Carolien Visser (@cavideo)



“I’mqueerand I’m attracted to female bodies, but I don’t expect or enjoy seeing female athletes or male athletes put in a position to battle self-consciousness at their place of work.

“That is not part of the job description. I lived that life and know that excellence is born of unselfconsciousness, of freedom, and embodiment of action and instinct.

“Stop making it harder for half the population.”

Tweet questioning a Dutch swimmer's choice of revealing swim trunks at the 2024 Olympics.



Tweet by Ian M sharing thoughts on Dutch swimmer's revealing trunks in 2024 Olympics.



Tweet referencing Dutch art influence on Aboriginal dot painting.



Comment comparing Dutch swimmer's trunks to toddler swimwear.



Comment on Dutch swimmer's revealing trunks at 2024 Olympics, questioning comfort and performance legality.



Belinda Tye comments on Dutch swimmer's revealing trunks with a laughing emoji.



Anita Foster comments on a design decision, calling it "Terrible" in black text on a white background.



Comment joke about Dutch swimmer's revealing trunks at 2024 Olympics.



"Comment saying 'It made me look twice' with a surprised emoji, discussing Dutch swimmer's revealing Olympic trunks.



Comment on Dutch swimmer's trunks during 2024 Olympics, humorously comparing with women's water polo.

Funny because this proves that men aren't actually privileged as feminists say. According to them, we live in a "patriarchy" where men are free to wear and show whatever they want while women are oppressed and their bodies are censored because they are "objectified". However, this shows the contrary: men are the ones who can't even give the impression they are revealing more than they "should". And while a lot of people (especially, women and queer men) opt to hypersexualize and objectify, others claim for censorship because it's "inappropriate" that men show that much of skin.

Jesus Christ what is this, a who-has-it-worse competition? The fact is that practically all major roles in the world (including those responsible for athlete dresscodes) are occupied by men so feel free to complain there if you disagree with something. A perfect example of a double standard is that women can be arrested for walking around topless since that counts as “indecent exposure” while it’s perfectly acceptable for men to walk around without a shirt.

dump woke

This comment is hidden.Click here to view.

Exactly who do you think "forces" women to wear the teeny tiny bikini bottoms when playing beach volleyball? I've never heard a single complaint by the female players or by the audience, so this is a win-win. The tiny bikinis have not only improved the capabilities of the athletes because they're not encumbered by bulkiness, but surely have improved TV ratings as well. Their "female-ness" is covered, leaving the viewer to wonder what's underneath, and their suits are not transparent. In the case of the man wearing swim trunks that are transparent enough to show his genitals, that was a huge mistake to allow it, in my opinion. The photo clearly shows his genitals, which I don't believe has ever been allowed before and shouldn't be allowed now. All for publicity and TV ratings!

Why must we always reduce these incredible athletes to their appearances, male or female? It's so juvenile. Lets not talk about his amazing swimming prowess, no, let's talk about his BUM BUM like fecking children.

because they are reduce them self already

He's obviously asking for publicity, good or bad. "Any publicity is better than no publicity" as the saying goes. That particular men's swimsuit by Speedo could have been manufactured in such a way to be as "good" for the athletics, and still not be transparent in the front. I think it was a poor decision to allow it in the Olympics. Think about the female athletes, primarily swimmers and gymnasts, who wear as little as possible when competing. You don't hear any complaints, but as small as their attire is, it's still NOT transparent.

Hell, I'm looking at his outfit and it's NOT transparent. It's just a storm in a teacup.

title: Dutch Swimmer Goes Viral For Super Revealing Trunks During 2024 Olympics: “Is This Legal?” | Bored Panda
description: Dutch swimmer Arno Kamminga stunned in his mesh trunks during the 2024 Paris Olympic Games. World
